ChangeRequest.name: Optional enumerated attributes in derived data entity types
ChangeRequest.comment:
<!-- See: documentation on [Issues in GitLab](https://gitlab.sofa.dev/sdd-bird-team/bird-logical-data-model/-/wikis/Issues-in-GitLab) -->
# Overview
Given the currently applied convention for optionality of enumerated attributes - which is btw in contrast to Codd's 'systematic treatment of null values' - it seems that the optionality of some enumerated attributes in derived data entity types is incorrect.
For example, the attribute `Financial asset instrument derived data`.`Performing status` is optional although it is an enumerated attribute.
Please note that this situation is present in other locations in the model as well, for example `Balance sheet recognised financial asset instrument derived data`.`Transfer between impairment stages` - this might be a systematic issue (check forward engineering scripts).
### Contact person
dominik.lin-ully@oenb.at
### Background information _[info]_
### Points/Questions to resolve
* please reflect on the treatment of null values * if the given convention is considered appropriate and there is a good reason to violate the 'systematic treatment of null values', please apply the given convention consistently * if the given convention is considered appropriate, maybe explore the possibility to implement validations (e.g., using SQL data modeler's validation functionality) to avoid future inconsistencies
### Related issues
### Before closing checklist - [ ] Assignee for review - [ ] Assignee to close - [ ] Other actions
